# Polarization Interventions

This project area focuses on reducing political polarization through shared social identities. The aim is to understand when common ground can reduce hostility, improve cooperation, or support public health and civic behavior.

## Core question

Which shared identities can reduce affective polarization without flattening real disagreement or asking people to suppress important commitments?

## Possible designs

- Shared identity message experiments
- Public health communication studies
- Cross-partisan perception studies
- Tests of identity salience, authenticity, and behavioral intention

## Next actions

- Compare candidate shared identities.
- Identify measures of affective polarization and cooperation.
- Develop intervention materials.
- Link this project to the broader identity and public health literature.
